trans-unit.source: 🚧This part of training platform is under ⚠️construction⚠️ and may not be shared or published! 🚧
trans-unit.target: 🚧This part of training platform is under ⚠️construction⚠️ and may not be shared or published! 🚧

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.
trans-unit.target: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.

trans-unit.source: :::{card} :class-card: sd-border-1 sd-shadow-none __Aim of the exercise:__ Aina, the GIS expert at the Malagasy Red Cross (CRM), is preparing for the upcoming cyclone season. She wants to improve her team’s ability to act quickly once a storm is forecasted by automating key analyses in QGIS. These include estimating exposed populations, identifying impacted services like health and education, and assessing whether health posts can be reached from key warehouses within a critical 10-hour window. The goal is to prepare an end-to-end analysis and visualization workflow that can support fast, data-driven anticipatory action before a cyclone makes landfall. ^^^
trans-unit.target: :::{card} :class-card: sd-border-1 sd-shadow-none __Aim of the exercise:__ Aina, the GIS expert at the Malagasy Red Cross (CRM), is preparing for the upcoming cyclone season. She wants to improve her team’s ability to act quickly once a storm is forecasted by automating key analyses in QGIS. These include estimating exposed populations, identifying impacted services like health and education, and assessing whether health posts can be reached from key warehouses within a critical 10-hour window. The goal is to prepare an end-to-end analysis and visualization workflow that can support fast, data-driven anticipatory action before a cyclone makes landfall. ^^^

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on training conduction
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on training conduction

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: __Download all datasets [here](), save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__
trans-unit.target: __Download all datasets [here](), save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__

trans-unit.source: The folder is called “ and contains the whole [standard folder structure](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.md#standard-folder-structure) with all data in the input folder and the additional documentation in the documentation folder.
trans-unit.target: The folder is called “ and contains the whole [standard folder structure](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.md#standard-folder-structure) with all data in the input folder and the additional documentation in the documentation folder.

trans-unit.source: The administrative boundaries on level 0-4 for Madagascar can be accessed via HDX provided by OCHA. For this trigger mechanism we provide the administrative boundaries on level 1 (regional level) and 2 (district level) as a shapefile.
trans-unit.target: The administrative boundaries on level 0-4 for Madagascar can be accessed via HDX provided by OCHA. For this trigger mechanism we provide the administrative boundaries on level 1 (regional level) and 2 (district level) as a shapefile.

trans-unit.source: [International Best Track Archive for Climate Stewardship (IBTrACS)](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/international-best-track-archive)
trans-unit.target: [International Best Track Archive for Climate Stewardship (IBTrACS)](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/international-best-track-archive)

trans-unit.source: IBTrACS project is the most complete global collection of tropical cyclones available. It merges recent and historical tropical cyclone data from multiple agencies to create a unified, publicly available, best-track dataset that improves inter-agency comparisons.
trans-unit.target: IBTrACS project is the most complete global collection of tropical cyclones available. It merges recent and historical tropical cyclone data from multiple agencies to create a unified, publicly available, best-track dataset that improves inter-agency comparisons.

trans-unit.source: The POI data (education facilities and health sites) is downloaded using the HOT Export Tool based on OpenStreetMap data.
trans-unit.target: The POI data (education facilities and health sites) is downloaded using the HOT Export Tool based on OpenStreetMap data.

trans-unit.source: The worldpop dataset in raster format provides the estimated total number of people per grid-cell for the year 2020. We will be working with the Constrained Individual countries 2020 dataset at a resolution of 100m.
trans-unit.target: The worldpop dataset in raster format provides the estimated total number of people per grid-cell for the year 2020. We will be working with the Constrained Individual countries 2020 dataset at a resolution of 100m.

trans-unit.source: **Aina** is the GIS expert at the **Croix-Rouge Malagasy (CRM)**. With the cyclone season approaching, she knows that time is of the essence once a storm is forecasted. Every hour counts when it comes to protecting communities at risk.
trans-unit.target: **Aina** is the GIS expert at the **Croix-Rouge Malagasy (CRM)**. With the cyclone season approaching, she knows that time is of the essence once a storm is forecasted. Every hour counts when it comes to protecting communities at risk.

trans-unit.source: This year, Aina wants to be one step ahead. Instead of manually analyzing cyclone data under pressure, she decides to **prepare an automated QGIS model** that will help her respond quickly and efficiently.
trans-unit.target: This year, Aina wants to be one step ahead. Instead of manually analyzing cyclone data under pressure, she decides to **prepare an automated QGIS model** that will help her respond quickly and efficiently.

trans-unit.source: Build a workflow that automatically estimates exposed populations and infrastructure at risk.
trans-unit.target: Build a workflow that automatically estimates exposed populations and infrastructure at risk.

trans-unit.source: Task 1: Estimating Exposed Population – Aina’s Manual Approach
trans-unit.target: Task 1: Estimating Exposed Population – Aina’s Manual Approach

trans-unit.source: Before developing the automated model, Aina used to estimate the exposed population manually whenever a cyclone approached Madagascar. In this task, you will follow the steps she used in the past by working with the historical track of **Cyclone Harald**, WorldPop raster data, and administrative boundaries.
trans-unit.target: Before developing the automated model, Aina used to estimate the exposed population manually whenever a cyclone approached Madagascar. In this task, you will follow the steps she used in the past by working with the historical track of **Cyclone Harald**, WorldPop raster data, and administrative boundaries.

trans-unit.source: You will manually buffer the cyclone track, clip the population raster, and calculate exposed population using zonal statistics.
trans-unit.target: You will manually buffer the cyclone track, clip the population raster, and calculate exposed population using zonal statistics.

trans-unit.source: **Open QGIS** and create a [new project](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.md#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` -> `New`
trans-unit.target: **Open QGIS** and create a [new project](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.md#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` -> `New`

trans-unit.source: **Save the project** in the “project” folder. To do that click on `Project` -> `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Name the project “Cyclon_Harald_Exposure”.
trans-unit.target: **Save the project** in the “project” folder. To do that click on `Project` -> `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Name the project “Cyclon_Harald_Exposure”.

trans-unit.source: **Load the GeoJOSN** file "example_Harald_2025_Track.geojson" in your project by drag and drop ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)) . Open the folder `data` -> `input`
trans-unit.target: **Load the GeoJOSN** file "example_Harald_2025_Track.geojson" in your project by drag and drop ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)) . Open the folder `data` -> `input`

trans-unit.source: **Reproject the cyclone track** to use meters instead of degrees (important for accurate buffering):
trans-unit.target: **Reproject the cyclone track** to use meters instead of degrees (important for accurate buffering):

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Reproject Layer`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Reproject Layer`.

trans-unit.source: Target CRS: `EPSG:29738` or another meter-based CRS appropriate for Madagascar.
trans-unit.target: Target CRS: `EPSG:29738` or another meter-based CRS appropriate for Madagascar.

trans-unit.source: Save the result in the `temp` folder as: **`Harald_Track_Reprojected`**
trans-unit.target: Save the result in the `temp` folder as: **`Harald_Track_Reprojected`**

trans-unit.source: Save output in the `temp` folder as: **`Harald_Buffer_200km`**
trans-unit.target: Save output in the `temp` folder as: **`Harald_Buffer_200km`**

trans-unit.source: ::: 6. **Reproject the buffer back to EPSG:4326 (to match the raster's CRS):**
trans-unit.target: ::: 6. **Reproject the buffer back to EPSG:4326 (to match the raster's CRS):**

trans-unit.source: In the Processing Toolbox, search for Reproject Layer.
trans-unit.target: In the Processing Toolbox, search for Reproject Layer.

trans-unit.source: Save the output in the temp folder as: Harald_Buffer_200km_4326
trans-unit.target: Save the output in the temp folder as: Harald_Buffer_200km_4326

trans-unit.source: Add using drag and drop or `Add Vector Layer`.
trans-unit.target: Add using drag and drop or `Add Vector Layer`.

trans-unit.source: **Clip the population raster** using the buffered impact zone:
trans-unit.target: **Clip the population raster** using the buffered impact zone:

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Clip Raster by Mask Layer`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Clip Raster by Mask Layer`.

trans-unit.source: Save output in the `temp` folder as: **`Harald_Pop_Clip`**
trans-unit.target: Save output in the `temp` folder as: **`Harald_Pop_Clip`**

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Intermediate Result: Clip Population Raster Alyer
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Intermediate Result: Clip Population Raster Alyer

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Zonal Statistics`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Zonal Statistics`.

trans-unit.source: Save the updated vector layer in the `result` folder as: **`Harald_Exposed_Populationg`**
trans-unit.target: Save the updated vector layer in the `result` folder as: **`Harald_Exposed_Populationg`**

trans-unit.source: The result will be a new column in the attribute table of the `mdg_admbnda_adm2_BNGRC_OCHA_201810312.gpkg` layer, showing the total population within the cyclone buffer per district.
trans-unit.target: The result will be a new column in the attribute table of the `mdg_admbnda_adm2_BNGRC_OCHA_201810312.gpkg` layer, showing the total population within the cyclone buffer per district.

trans-unit.source: **Visualise the affected population by classifying the results**: Now that Aina has estimated the exposed population in each district, she wants to clearly show the differences across regions on the map. To do this, we'll apply a **graduated classification** to the `Harald_Exposed_Population` layer using the new population field created by the Zonal Statistics tool.
trans-unit.target: **Visualise the affected population by classifying the results**: Now that Aina has estimated the exposed population in each district, she wants to clearly show the differences across regions on the map. To do this, we'll apply a **graduated classification** to the `Harald_Exposed_Population` layer using the new population field created by the Zonal Statistics tool.

trans-unit.source: In the **Layers panel**, right-click on the layer `Harald_Exposed_Population` and choose `Properties`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Layers panel**, right-click on the layer `Harald_Exposed_Population` and choose `Properties`.

trans-unit.source: Go to the **Symbology** tab on the left.
trans-unit.target: Go to the **Symbology** tab on the left.

trans-unit.source: At the top of the window, change the style from `Single Symbol` to `Graduated`.
trans-unit.target: At the top of the window, change the style from `Single Symbol` to `Graduated`.

trans-unit.source: In the **Value** drop-down menu, select the field that contains the population sum. It typically starts with the prefix you defined earlier, e.g. `exposed_population_sum`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Value** drop-down menu, select the field that contains the population sum. It typically starts with the prefix you defined earlier, e.g. `exposed_population_sum`.

trans-unit.source: Set the **color ramp** to one that suits your map (e.g. `Reds`).
trans-unit.target: Set the **color ramp** to one that suits your map (e.g. `Reds`).

trans-unit.source: Choose a **classification mode** (e.g. `Quantile`, `Natural Breaks`, or `Equal Interval`) and select the number of classes (e.g. 5).
trans-unit.target: Choose a **classification mode** (e.g. `Quantile`, `Natural Breaks`, or `Equal Interval`) and select the number of classes (e.g. 5).

trans-unit.source: Click `Apply` and then `OK` to display the classified map.
trans-unit.target: Click `Apply` and then `OK` to display the classified map.

trans-unit.source: Your results should look something like this:
trans-unit.target: Your results should look something like this:

trans-unit.source: Task 2: Automation of Exposed Population Estimation – Aina's Model
trans-unit.target: Task 2: Automation of Exposed Population Estimation – Aina's Model

trans-unit.source: After manually estimating exposed populations in past cyclone seasons, Aina has decided to prepare an **automated model** using the **QGIS Graphical Modeller**. This will help her move faster and avoid repeating the same steps manually each time a cyclone is forecasted.
trans-unit.target: After manually estimating exposed populations in past cyclone seasons, Aina has decided to prepare an **automated model** using the **QGIS Graphical Modeller**. This will help her move faster and avoid repeating the same steps manually each time a cyclone is forecasted.

trans-unit.source: In this task, you will help Aina build a simple version of that model using the tools from Task 1. The model should:
trans-unit.target: In this task, you will help Aina build a simple version of that model using the tools from Task 1. The model should:

trans-unit.source: Reproject the cyclone track to EPSG:29738
trans-unit.target: Reproject the cyclone track to EPSG:29738

trans-unit.source: Reproject the buffer back to EPSG:4326
trans-unit.target: Reproject the buffer back to EPSG:4326

trans-unit.source: Run Zonal Statistics to get exposed population per district
trans-unit.target: Run Zonal Statistics to get exposed population per district

trans-unit.source: Open the **Graphical Modeler** from the top menu: `Processing` → `Graphical Modeler…`
trans-unit.target: Open the **Graphical Modeler** from the top menu: `Processing` → `Graphical Modeler…`

trans-unit.source: A new model window will open. On the **left side**, click on **`Model Properties`** to define basic information about the model:
trans-unit.target: A new model window will open. On the **left side**, click on **`Model Properties`** to define basic information about the model:

trans-unit.source: Leave the description empty or write: _“Automated model to estimate exposed population based on cyclone buffer.”_
trans-unit.target: Leave the description empty or write: _“Automated model to estimate exposed population based on cyclone buffer.”_

trans-unit.source: Click the **Save** icon (💾) or go to `Model` → `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Click the **Save** icon (💾) or go to `Model` → `Save`.

trans-unit.source: Navigate to the **`models` folder** of your training structure.
trans-unit.target: Navigate to the **`models` folder** of your training structure.

trans-unit.source: On the **left panel**, expand the **Inputs** section.
trans-unit.target: On the **left panel**, expand the **Inputs** section.

trans-unit.source: Add the following input layers with type constraints:
trans-unit.target: Add the following input layers with type constraints:

trans-unit.source: In the **Advanced panel**, set **geometry type** to `Line`
trans-unit.target: In the **Advanced panel**, set **geometry type** to `Line`

trans-unit.source: In the **Advanced panel**, set **geometry type** to `Polygon`
trans-unit.target: In the **Advanced panel**, set **geometry type** to `Polygon`

trans-unit.source: These will appear at the top of your model canvas and serve as the input data when the model is run.
trans-unit.target: These will appear at the top of your model canvas and serve as the input data when the model is run.

trans-unit.source: From the **Algorithms** panel, search for **Reproject Layer** .
trans-unit.target: From the **Algorithms** panel, search for **Reproject Layer** .

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Reprojecter la couche de trajectoire du cyclone a EPSG : 29738`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Reprojecter la couche de trajectoire du cyclone a EPSG : 29738`

trans-unit.source: Set **Target CRS** to `EPSG:29738 – Madagascar / Laborde Grid`.
trans-unit.target: Set **Target CRS** to `EPSG:29738 – Madagascar / Laborde Grid`.

trans-unit.source: Set the output to **Model Output** (leave the output name **empty**).
trans-unit.target: Set the output to **Model Output** (leave the output name **empty**).

trans-unit.source: Click `OK` to add the step to the model.
trans-unit.target: Click `OK` to add the step to the model.

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Mettre en mémoire tampon la couche Cyclone reprojetée`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Mettre en mémoire tampon la couche Cyclone reprojetée`

trans-unit.source: Set **Input layer** to the output from the previous step (from **Algorithm Output**).
trans-unit.target: Set **Input layer** to the output from the previous step (from **Algorithm Output**).

trans-unit.source: Set the output to **Model Output** (leave the output name **empty**).
trans-unit.target: Set the output to **Model Output** (leave the output name **empty**).

trans-unit.source: Click `OK` to add the step to the model.
trans-unit.target: Click `OK` to add the step to the model.

trans-unit.source: From the **Algorithms** panel, search for **Reproject Layer**.
trans-unit.target: From the **Algorithms** panel, search for **Reproject Layer**.

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Reprojecter le tampon vers EPSG:4326`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Reprojecter le tampon vers EPSG:4326`

trans-unit.source: Set **Input layer** to the output from the previous step (from **Algorithm Output**).
trans-unit.target: Set **Input layer** to the output from the previous step (from **Algorithm Output**).

trans-unit.source: Set the output to **Model Output** (leave the output name **empty**).
trans-unit.target: Set the output to **Model Output** (leave the output name **empty**).

trans-unit.source: Click `OK` to add the step to the model.
trans-unit.target: Click `OK` to add the step to the model.

trans-unit.source: **Clip the population raster using the buffered area**
trans-unit.target: **Clip the population raster using the buffered area**

trans-unit.source: From the **Algorithms** panel, search for **Clip Raster by Mask Layer** .
trans-unit.target: From the **Algorithms** panel, search for **Clip Raster by Mask Layer** .

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Découper la couche raster de population pour l'étendre au tampon Cyclon`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Découper la couche raster de population pour l'étendre au tampon Cyclon`

trans-unit.source: Set **Mask layer** to the output from the previous step (from **Algorithm Output**).
trans-unit.target: Set **Mask layer** to the output from the previous step (from **Algorithm Output**).

trans-unit.source: Set the output to **Model Output** (leave the output name **empty**).
trans-unit.target: Set the output to **Model Output** (leave the output name **empty**).

trans-unit.source: Click `OK` to add the step to the model.
trans-unit.target: Click `OK` to add the step to the model.

trans-unit.source: **Calculate zonal statistics to estimate exposed population**
trans-unit.target: **Calculate zonal statistics to estimate exposed population**

trans-unit.source: From the **Algorithms** panel, search for **Zonal Statistics** .
trans-unit.target: From the **Algorithms** panel, search for **Zonal Statistics** .

trans-unit.source: In the configuration window: Calcul de la population exposée aux cyclones par district
trans-unit.target: In the configuration window: Calcul de la population exposée aux cyclones par district

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Calcul de la population exposée aux cyclones par district`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Calcul de la population exposée aux cyclones par district`

trans-unit.source: Set **Raster layer** to the output of the previous step (from **Algorithm Output**).
trans-unit.target: Set **Raster layer** to the output of the previous step (from **Algorithm Output**).

trans-unit.source: Set the output to **Model Output** and name it:
trans-unit.target: Set the output to **Model Output** and name it:

trans-unit.source: Click `OK` to add the step to the model.
trans-unit.target: Click `OK` to add the step to the model.

trans-unit.source: **Your results should look something like this:**
trans-unit.target: **Your results should look something like this:**

trans-unit.source: Before saving or running, click the ✔️ **Validate Model** button in the top toolbar.
trans-unit.target: Before saving or running, click the ✔️ **Validate Model** button in the top toolbar.

trans-unit.source: Fix any warnings or errors shown in the log panel.
trans-unit.target: Fix any warnings or errors shown in the log panel.

trans-unit.source: This helps ensure your model is complete and won't break during execution.
trans-unit.target: This helps ensure your model is complete and won't break during execution.

trans-unit.source: Run the model by clicking on `Model` -> `Run Model`
trans-unit.target: Run the model by clicking on `Model` -> `Run Model`

trans-unit.source: Set the model output **exposed_population_sum** to `Harald_Exposed_Population`and save it in the `data` -> `output`
trans-unit.target: Set the model output **exposed_population_sum** to `Harald_Exposed_Population`and save it in the `data` -> `output`

trans-unit.source: You can now run this model any time a new cyclone track becomes available.
trans-unit.target: You can now run this model any time a new cyclone track becomes available.

trans-unit.source: **Your results should look something like this:**
trans-unit.target: **Your results should look something like this:**

trans-unit.source: **Add the cyclone buffer as an additional model output**
trans-unit.target: **Add the cyclone buffer as an additional model output**

trans-unit.source: Double-click on the algorithm from step 7 (**Reproject the buffer back to EPSG:4326**) to open its configuration.
trans-unit.target: Double-click on the algorithm from step 7 (**Reproject the buffer back to EPSG:4326**) to open its configuration.

trans-unit.source: In the **Output layer** field, check the box for **Model Output**.
trans-unit.target: In the **Output layer** field, check the box for **Model Output**.

trans-unit.source: Give the output a clear name, for example:
trans-unit.target: Give the output a clear name, for example:

trans-unit.source: This will allow the model to produce both the exposed population results and the buffered cyclone impact zone when it is run.
trans-unit.target: This will allow the model to produce both the exposed population results and the buffered cyclone impact zone when it is run.

trans-unit.source: Run the model by clicking on `Model` -> `Run Model`
trans-unit.target: Run the model by clicking on `Model` -> `Run Model`

trans-unit.source: Set the model output **cyclone_harald_buffer** to `cyclone_harald_buffer`and save it in the `data` -> `output`
trans-unit.target: Set the model output **cyclone_harald_buffer** to `cyclone_harald_buffer`and save it in the `data` -> `output`

trans-unit.source: Set the model output **exposed_population_sum** to `Harald_Exposed_Population`and save it in the `data` -> `output`
trans-unit.target: Set the model output **exposed_population_sum** to `Harald_Exposed_Population`and save it in the `data` -> `output`

trans-unit.source: Definition of the model input: Admin Bounderies :::
trans-unit.target: Definition of the model input: Admin Bounderies :::

trans-unit.source: Task 3: Identifying Affected Health Facilities and Schools – Aina Adds More Layers
trans-unit.target: Task 3: Identifying Affected Health Facilities and Schools – Aina Adds More Layers

trans-unit.source: After building her model to estimate exposed population, Aina wants to expand its usefulness. She decides to also **identify critical services** affected by cyclones — especially **health facilities** and **schools**.
trans-unit.target: After building her model to estimate exposed population, Aina wants to expand its usefulness. She decides to also **identify critical services** affected by cyclones — especially **health facilities** and **schools**.

trans-unit.source: Not only does she want to know *which* facilities are affected, but also *how many in total exist* per district. That way, she can calculate the **percentage of services affected** in each area.
trans-unit.target: Not only does she want to know *which* facilities are affected, but also *how many in total exist* per district. That way, she can calculate the **percentage of services affected** in each area.

trans-unit.source: To achieve this, she will use two point datasets from OpenStreetMap:
trans-unit.target: To achieve this, she will use two point datasets from OpenStreetMap:

trans-unit.source: **Load the health and education facilities datasets** First, let's have a look at the data we want to work with.
trans-unit.target: **Load the health and education facilities datasets** First, let's have a look at the data we want to work with.

trans-unit.source: Drag and drop the following layers into your QGIS project:
trans-unit.target: Drag and drop the following layers into your QGIS project:

trans-unit.source: Confirm that both layers are visible in the **Layers Panel**
trans-unit.target: Confirm that both layers are visible in the **Layers Panel**

trans-unit.source: **Save your model under a new name**
trans-unit.target: **Save your model under a new name**

trans-unit.source: Immediately save it under a new name:
trans-unit.target: Immediately save it under a new name:

trans-unit.source: Save it to the `project` folder as:
trans-unit.target: Save it to the `project` folder as:

trans-unit.source: From the **Algorithms** panel, search for **Count Points in Polygon**.
trans-unit.target: From the **Algorithms** panel, search for **Count Points in Polygon**.

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Comptez le nombre d'établissements de santé dans chaque district.`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Comptez le nombre d'établissements de santé dans chaque district.`

trans-unit.source: Add another **Count Points in Polygon** step.
trans-unit.target: Add another **Count Points in Polygon** step.

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Comptez le nombre d'établissements de education dans chaque district`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Comptez le nombre d'établissements de education dans chaque district`

trans-unit.source: **Overlay layer**: buffered cyclone zone (use “Reprojected to EPSG:4326” from **Algorithm Output**)
trans-unit.target: **Overlay layer**: buffered cyclone zone (use “Reprojected to EPSG:4326” from **Algorithm Output**)

trans-unit.source: **Overlay layer**: buffered cyclone zone (use “Reprojected to EPSG:4326” from **Algorithm Output**)
trans-unit.target: **Overlay layer**: buffered cyclone zone (use “Reprojected to EPSG:4326” from **Algorithm Output**)

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Compter les établissements de santé touchés par district`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Compter les établissements de santé touchés par district`

trans-unit.source: Add a description: Compter les établissements de santé touchés par district
trans-unit.target: Add a description: Compter les établissements de santé touchés par district

trans-unit.source: **Polygon layer**: Count total health facilities output
trans-unit.target: **Polygon layer**: Count total health facilities output

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Compter les établissements education touchés par district`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Compter les établissements education touchés par district`

trans-unit.source: **Polygon layer**: Count total education facilities output
trans-unit.target: **Polygon layer**: Count total education facilities output

trans-unit.source: **Calculate percentage of affected Health Facilities** To compute the percentage of affected health sites per administrative area, we will use the **Field Calculator**:
trans-unit.target: **Calculate percentage of affected Health Facilities** To compute the percentage of affected health sites per administrative area, we will use the **Field Calculator**:

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Calculer le pourcentage d’établissements de santé touchés par district`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Calculer le pourcentage d’établissements de santé touchés par district`

trans-unit.source: **Input layer**: the output of Count Affected Health Facilities per Admin 2
trans-unit.target: **Input layer**: the output of Count Affected Health Facilities per Admin 2

trans-unit.source: **Calculate percentage of affected Education Facilities** To compute the percentage of affected education sites per administrative area, we will use the **Field Calculator**:
trans-unit.target: **Calculate percentage of affected Education Facilities** To compute the percentage of affected education sites per administrative area, we will use the **Field Calculator**:

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Calculer le pourcentage d’établissements d’éducation touchés par district`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Calculer le pourcentage d’établissements d’éducation touchés par district`

trans-unit.source: **Input layer**: the output of Count Affected Education Facilities per Admin 2
trans-unit.target: **Input layer**: the output of Count Affected Education Facilities per Admin 2

trans-unit.source: Click the ✔️ **Validate Model** button to check for errors.
trans-unit.target: Click the ✔️ **Validate Model** button to check for errors.

trans-unit.source: Click the ▶️ `Run` button in the top-right corner of the Graphical Modeler window.
trans-unit.target: Click the ▶️ `Run` button in the top-right corner of the Graphical Modeler window.

trans-unit.source: Click on the three dots for each input dataset and select the correct input:
trans-unit.target: Click on the three dots for each input dataset and select the correct input:

trans-unit.source: `Cyclone Track` → select the GeoJSON of the storm path (e.g. `Harald_2025_Track.geojson`)
trans-unit.target: `Cyclone Track` → select the GeoJSON of the storm path (e.g. `Harald_2025_Track.geojson`)

trans-unit.source: `Population Raster` → select the WorldPop raster file
trans-unit.target: `Population Raster` → select the WorldPop raster file

trans-unit.source: `Health Facilities` → select the point dataset for health sites
trans-unit.target: `Health Facilities` → select the point dataset for health sites

trans-unit.source: `Education Facilities` → select the point dataset for schools
trans-unit.target: `Education Facilities` → select the point dataset for schools

trans-unit.source: Save all output layers in the output folder and use the names below.
trans-unit.target: Save all output layers in the output folder and use the names below.

trans-unit.source: Click `Run` to execute the full model.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run` to execute the full model.

trans-unit.source: Task 4: Visualizing Cyclone Impact Results – Aina Styles Her Layers
trans-unit.target: Task 4: Visualizing Cyclone Impact Results – Aina Styles Her Layers

trans-unit.source: Aina now has all the analysis results she needs — but numbers and tables alone won’t convince her colleagues or decision-makers. What they need are clear and easy-to-read maps that can be used directly in meetings and reports.
trans-unit.target: Aina now has all the analysis results she needs — but numbers and tables alone won’t convince her colleagues or decision-makers. What they need are clear and easy-to-read maps that can be used directly in meetings and reports.

trans-unit.source: To save time, Aina doesn’t want to adjust colors and legends manually each time a new cyclone comes in. Instead, she will use ready-made style files (.qml) that instantly give layers a professional and consistent look. Where no style exists yet, she will create one herself, so that next time the map can be updated with just a few clicks.
trans-unit.target: To save time, Aina doesn’t want to adjust colors and legends manually each time a new cyclone comes in. Instead, she will use ready-made style files (.qml) that instantly give layers a professional and consistent look. Where no style exists yet, she will create one herself, so that next time the map can be updated with just a few clicks.

trans-unit.source: In this task, you will help Aina make her cyclone impact maps both informative and visually compelling by applying and creating QGIS style files.
trans-unit.target: In this task, you will help Aina make her cyclone impact maps both informative and visually compelling by applying and creating QGIS style files.

trans-unit.source: Make sure the following layers are already loaded into your QGIS project. These are outputs from **Task 3**:
trans-unit.target: Make sure the following layers are already loaded into your QGIS project. These are outputs from **Task 3**:

trans-unit.source: Load them using **drag & drop** from your `results` folder, or
trans-unit.target: Load them using **drag & drop** from your `results` folder, or

trans-unit.source: Use `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer` or `Add Raster Layer`
trans-unit.target: Use `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer` or `Add Raster Layer`

trans-unit.source: Apply the following`.qml` style files to the respective layers:
trans-unit.target: Apply the following`.qml` style files to the respective layers:

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the layer in the `Layers Panel`.
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the layer in the `Layers Panel`.

trans-unit.source: In the window that opens, go to the `Symbology`tab.
trans-unit.target: In the window that opens, go to the `Symbology`tab.

trans-unit.source: At the bottom left, click `Style` → `Load style...`
trans-unit.target: At the bottom left, click `Style` → `Load style...`

trans-unit.source: Navigate to the corresponding `.qml` file in the folder `layer_sytle`and select it
trans-unit.target: Navigate to the corresponding `.qml` file in the folder `layer_sytle`and select it

trans-unit.source: 💡 *If the style doesn’t load correctly, double-check the column names and make sure the column name used in the `.qml` file matches the one in your layer. To do this, open the **Attribute Table** of the layer and compare field names.*
trans-unit.target: 💡 *If the style doesn’t load correctly, double-check the column names and make sure the column name used in the `.qml` file matches the one in your layer. To do this, open the **Attribute Table** of the layer and compare field names.*

trans-unit.source: Aina also wants to visualise the percentage of exposed health and education facilities. However, since there is no prepared style available, she must complete the process manually.
trans-unit.target: Aina also wants to visualise the percentage of exposed health and education facilities. However, since there is no prepared style available, she must complete the process manually.

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the layer in the **Layers Panel**
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the layer in the **Layers Panel**

trans-unit.source: In the window that opens, go to the **Symbology** tab
trans-unit.target: In the window that opens, go to the **Symbology** tab

trans-unit.source: Open the **Histogram** tab to view the value distribution by clicking on `calculate histogram`
trans-unit.target: Open the **Histogram** tab to view the value distribution by clicking on `calculate histogram`

trans-unit.source: Next go back to `Classes` and set the following configuration:
trans-unit.target: Next go back to `Classes` and set the following configuration:

trans-unit.source: Choose a color ramp (e.g., light yellow → dark red)
trans-unit.target: Choose a color ramp (e.g., light yellow → dark red)

trans-unit.source: Optionally customize class labels for clarity
trans-unit.target: Optionally customize class labels for clarity

trans-unit.source: Repeat the same process for the layer `admin2_education_affected`. After duplicating the layer, rename the new one to:
trans-unit.target: Repeat the same process for the layer `admin2_education_affected`. After duplicating the layer, rename the new one to:

trans-unit.source: 🧠 *Why 4 equal classes?* This helps visualize severity across districts using simple and interpretable risk categories. However, you can experiment with **Natural Breaks** if data is unevenly distributed.
trans-unit.target: 🧠 *Why 4 equal classes?* This helps visualize severity across districts using simple and interpretable risk categories. However, you can experiment with **Natural Breaks** if data is unevenly distributed.

trans-unit.source: Save your manually created styles as `.qml` files for future reuse.
trans-unit.target: Save your manually created styles as `.qml` files for future reuse.

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the layer in the **Layers Panel**
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the layer in the **Layers Panel**

trans-unit.source: In the window that opens, go to the **Symbology** tab
trans-unit.target: In the window that opens, go to the **Symbology** tab

trans-unit.source: Save the file in the folder `layer_sytle`
trans-unit.target: Save the file in the folder `layer_sytle`

trans-unit.source: 5. *(Optional)* Import Styles into Your QGIS Library
trans-unit.target: 5. *(Optional)* Import Styles into Your QGIS Library

trans-unit.source: To reuse your styles in any future project:
trans-unit.target: To reuse your styles in any future project:

trans-unit.source: Browse to and select your saved `.qml` files
trans-unit.target: Browse to and select your saved `.qml` files

trans-unit.source: The styles will now appear as presets in the **Layer Styling Panel**.
trans-unit.target: The styles will now appear as presets in the **Layer Styling Panel**.

trans-unit.source: Task 5: Quick Map Creation – Aina Uses Map Templates
trans-unit.target: Task 5: Quick Map Creation – Aina Uses Map Templates

trans-unit.source: After all the hard work of analyzing data and styling layers, Aina is ready to **share her results**. But creating a professional-looking map from scratch every time would be slow and repetitive.
trans-unit.target: After all the hard work of analyzing data and styling layers, Aina is ready to **share her results**. But creating a professional-looking map from scratch every time would be slow and repetitive.

trans-unit.source: To save time, she uses **map templates (.qpt files)** prepared by her team. These templates already contain the essential elements — map frames, legends, logos, titles, and scale bars. With them, Aina can turn her analysis into a **clean, consistent map** in just a few clicks.
trans-unit.target: To save time, she uses **map templates (.qpt files)** prepared by her team. These templates already contain the essential elements — map frames, legends, logos, titles, and scale bars. With them, Aina can turn her analysis into a **clean, consistent map** in just a few clicks.

trans-unit.source: ✅ **Goal** Apply a ready-made QGIS map template to quickly create and export maps that show cyclone impacts on population, health facilities, and schools.
trans-unit.target: ✅ **Goal** Apply a ready-made QGIS map template to quickly create and export maps that show cyclone impacts on population, health facilities, and schools.

trans-unit.source: Load the pre-made print layout template
trans-unit.target: Load the pre-made print layout template

trans-unit.source: Locate the template `cyclone_impact_population_map_template.qpt` in your project folder under: `Map_Templates/`
trans-unit.target: Locate the template `cyclone_impact_population_map_template.qpt` in your project folder under: `Map_Templates/`

trans-unit.source: You can load the template **by drag-and-drop**:
trans-unit.target: You can load the template **by drag-and-drop**:

trans-unit.source: Drag the `.qpt` file directly into QGIS — a new layout will be created automatically.
trans-unit.target: Drag the `.qpt` file directly into QGIS — a new layout will be created automatically.

trans-unit.source: In the layout, go to `Layout` → `Import from Template…`
trans-unit.target: In the layout, go to `Layout` → `Import from Template…`

trans-unit.source: Right-click anywhere on the white canvas and choose `Page Properties`.
trans-unit.target: Right-click anywhere on the white canvas and choose `Page Properties`.

trans-unit.source: On the right-side panel, ensure the following:
trans-unit.target: On the right-side panel, ensure the following:

trans-unit.source: Update the attribute table of exposed districts
trans-unit.target: Update the attribute table of exposed districts

trans-unit.source: In the **Print Layout**, click on the attribute table (right-hand side of the layout).
trans-unit.target: In the **Print Layout**, click on the attribute table (right-hand side of the layout).

trans-unit.source: Ensure the correct layer is selected `Harald_Exposed_population`
trans-unit.target: Ensure the correct layer is selected `Harald_Exposed_population`

trans-unit.source: Click `Attributes…` → in the upper part under **Fields** click on `Clear`
trans-unit.target: Click `Attributes…` → in the upper part under **Fields** click on `Clear`

trans-unit.source: Then add the following layer by clicking on ➕ :
trans-unit.target: Then add the following layer by clicking on ➕ :

trans-unit.source: To sort the tabel content, under the **Sorting** clicking on ➕ and add the column `AMD1_EN`
trans-unit.target: To sort the tabel content, under the **Sorting** clicking on ➕ and add the column `AMD1_EN`

trans-unit.source: In the layout, click on the **Legend** item.
trans-unit.target: In the layout, click on the **Legend** item.

trans-unit.source: Scroll to **Legend items** and remove all entries (🗑️)
trans-unit.target: Scroll to **Legend items** and remove all entries (🗑️)

trans-unit.source: When selecting layers, check **Only visible layers**
trans-unit.target: When selecting layers, check **Only visible layers**

trans-unit.source: Rename legend entries to match layout naming
trans-unit.target: Rename legend entries to match layout naming

trans-unit.source: The logos that need to be added to the map are represented by the red **X**.
trans-unit.target: The logos that need to be added to the map are represented by the red **X**.

trans-unit.source: Click on the image in the **Item List**.
trans-unit.target: Click on the image in the **Item List**.

trans-unit.source: Click on the three dots ![](/fig/Three_points.png) next to the file path.
trans-unit.target: Click on the three dots ![](/fig/Three_points.png) next to the file path.

trans-unit.source: Browse to the folder `logos_pictures` and select the correct logo file.
trans-unit.target: Browse to the folder `logos_pictures` and select the correct logo file.

trans-unit.source: Review and update layout text elements
trans-unit.target: Review and update layout text elements

trans-unit.source: Make sure all text elements are up to date, especially:
trans-unit.target: Make sure all text elements are up to date, especially:

trans-unit.source: Adjust font size or alignment if necessary
trans-unit.target: Adjust font size or alignment if necessary

trans-unit.source: Task 6: Exporting Model Results for the Operations Team
trans-unit.target: Task 6: Exporting Model Results for the Operations Team

trans-unit.source: After producing maps and visuals, Aina often gets requests from the operations team:
trans-unit.target: After producing maps and visuals, Aina often gets requests from the operations team:

trans-unit.source: _“Can you send us the data in table format?”_
trans-unit.target: _“Can you send us the data in table format?”_

trans-unit.source: Instead of exporting these tables manually each time, Aina wants to automate this step within her model — ensuring that every run of the model produces clear, ready-to-use data files.
trans-unit.target: Instead of exporting these tables manually each time, Aina wants to automate this step within her model — ensuring that every run of the model produces clear, ready-to-use data files.

trans-unit.source: In this task, you’ll help Aina extend her existing model to export selected layers.
trans-unit.target: In this task, you’ll help Aina extend her existing model to export selected layers.

trans-unit.source: We will join the following layers step by step:
trans-unit.target: We will join the following layers step by step:

trans-unit.source: `admin2_health_affected_pct`: Contains the **total number of health facilities**, the **number of affected health facilities**, and the **percentage of affected health facilities**.
trans-unit.target: `admin2_health_affected_pct`: Contains the **total number of health facilities**, the **number of affected health facilities**, and the **percentage of affected health facilities**.

trans-unit.source: `admin2_education_affected_pct`: Contains the **total number of education facilities**, the **number of affected education facilities**, and the **percentage of affected education facilities**.
trans-unit.target: `admin2_education_affected_pct`: Contains the **total number of education facilities**, the **number of affected education facilities**, and the **percentage of affected education facilities**.

trans-unit.source: `exposed_population`: Contains the **total population per district** and the **exposed population** from the zonal statistics step.
trans-unit.target: `exposed_population`: Contains the **total population per district** and the **exposed population** from the zonal statistics step.

trans-unit.source: Join Health and Education data into one layer
trans-unit.target: Join Health and Education data into one layer

trans-unit.source: In the **Algorithms**, search for `Join Attributes by Field Value`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Algorithms**, search for `Join Attributes by Field Value`.

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Joindre santé et éducation dans une seule couche par ADM2`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Joindre santé et éducation dans une seule couche par ADM2`

trans-unit.source: **Layer 2 fields to copy**: Leave empty (all fields will be copied)
trans-unit.target: **Layer 2 fields to copy**: Leave empty (all fields will be copied)

trans-unit.source: **Join type**: Take attributes of the first matching feature only (one-to-one)
trans-unit.target: **Join type**: Take attributes of the first matching feature only (one-to-one)

trans-unit.source: Join the result with the population data Now join the result of the previous step (health + education) to the **exposed population** data.
trans-unit.target: Join the result with the population data Now join the result of the previous step (health + education) to the **exposed population** data.

trans-unit.source: Add a second `Join Attributes by Field Value` algorithm to the model
trans-unit.target: Add a second `Join Attributes by Field Value` algorithm to the model

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Joindre les données de population avec les indicateurs santé et éducation`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Joindre les données de population avec les indicateurs santé et éducation`

trans-unit.source: **Input Layer**: `exposed_population` (select from **Algorithm Output** of the Zonal Statistics step)
trans-unit.target: **Input Layer**: `exposed_population` (select from **Algorithm Output** of the Zonal Statistics step)

trans-unit.source: **Layer 2 fields to copy**: *(Enter the following field names exactly as shown — comma-separated, no spaces)*
trans-unit.target: **Layer 2 fields to copy**: *(Enter the following field names exactly as shown — comma-separated, no spaces)*

trans-unit.source: **Join type**: Take attributes of the first matching feature only (one-to-one)
trans-unit.target: **Join type**: Take attributes of the first matching feature only (one-to-one)

trans-unit.source: ::::{tip} Where to find the column names Open the **attribute tables** of the outputs `health_total_per_admin2`, `sum_exposed_healthsites_POI`, and `admin2_health_affected_pct` in QGIS. Look at the **column headers** to find the exact names of the fields you want to copy. :::: ::::{warning} Invisible spaces will break the join If a column name like `count_health_total` has an invisible trailing space, the join will silently fail. Always copy field names **directly from the attribute table** to avoid errors. ::::
trans-unit.target: ::::{tip} Where to find the column names Open the **attribute tables** of the outputs `health_total_per_admin2`, `sum_exposed_healthsites_POI`, and `admin2_health_affected_pct` in QGIS. Look at the **column headers** to find the exact names of the fields you want to copy. :::: ::::{warning} Invisible spaces will break the join If a column name like `count_health_total` has an invisible trailing space, the join will silently fail. Always copy field names **directly from the attribute table** to avoid errors. ::::

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Export to spreadsheet` and double-click to open.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Export to spreadsheet` and double-click to open.

trans-unit.source: Add a description: `Exporter les données de population, d'éducation et de santé dans un seul tableau`
trans-unit.target: Add a description: `Exporter les données de population, d'éducation et de santé dans un seul tableau`

trans-unit.source: **Input Layer**: Select the output of Step 3 from **Algorithm Output**
trans-unit.target: **Input Layer**: Select the output of Step 3 from **Algorithm Output**

trans-unit.source: Click `OK` to add it to the model. Once you run the model, this step will automatically generate a spreadsheet with all relevant indicators ready for the operations team!
trans-unit.target: Click `OK` to add it to the model. Once you run the model, this step will automatically generate a spreadsheet with all relevant indicators ready for the operations team!

trans-unit.source: Click the ✔️ **Validate Model** button to check for errors.
trans-unit.target: Click the ✔️ **Validate Model** button to check for errors.

trans-unit.source: Click the ▶️ `Run` button in the top-right corner of the Graphical Modeler window.
trans-unit.target: Click the ▶️ `Run` button in the top-right corner of the Graphical Modeler window.

trans-unit.source: Click on the three dots for each input dataset and select the correct input:
trans-unit.target: Click on the three dots for each input dataset and select the correct input:

trans-unit.source: `Cyclone Track` → select the GeoJSON of the storm path (e.g. `Harald_2025_Track.geojson`)
trans-unit.target: `Cyclone Track` → select the GeoJSON of the storm path (e.g. `Harald_2025_Track.geojson`)

trans-unit.source: `Population Raster` → select the WorldPop raster file
trans-unit.target: `Population Raster` → select the WorldPop raster file

trans-unit.source: `Health Facilities` → select the point dataset for health sites
trans-unit.target: `Health Facilities` → select the point dataset for health sites

trans-unit.source: `Education Facilities` → select the point dataset for schools
trans-unit.target: `Education Facilities` → select the point dataset for schools

trans-unit.source: Save all output layers in the output folder and use the names below.
trans-unit.target: Save all output layers in the output folder and use the names below.

trans-unit.source: Click `Run` to execute the full model.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run` to execute the full model.

trans-unit.source: Task 7: Reachability of health Posts from CRM Warehouses
trans-unit.target: Task 7: Reachability of health Posts from CRM Warehouses

trans-unit.source: When a cyclone is forecast to make landfall, Aina works with the logistics and health teams to decide **where to send prepositioned medical kits**. However, not all CRM warehouses stock the needed items — only three do.
trans-unit.target: When a cyclone is forecast to make landfall, Aina works with the logistics and health teams to decide **where to send prepositioned medical kits**. However, not all CRM warehouses stock the needed items — only three do.

trans-unit.source: To make fast, data-driven decisions, Aina wants to know **which health posts are reachable** from those warehouses **within 10 hours**. This analysis helps ensure that kits are sent to facilities **that can actually be reached in time**.
trans-unit.target: To make fast, data-driven decisions, Aina wants to know **which health posts are reachable** from those warehouses **within 10 hours**. This analysis helps ensure that kits are sent to facilities **that can actually be reached in time**.

trans-unit.source: Her goal is to create a clear visual map showing reachable vs. non-reachable health posts — and share this with decision-makers as quickly as possible.
trans-unit.target: Her goal is to create a clear visual map showing reachable vs. non-reachable health posts — and share this with decision-makers as quickly as possible.

trans-unit.source: 1. Filter Health Posts from the National Health Facility Dataset
trans-unit.target: 1. Filter Health Posts from the National Health Facility Dataset

trans-unit.source: Before checking which facilities are reachable, Aina needs to isolate **health posts** from the broader dataset of all health facilities.
trans-unit.target: Before checking which facilities are reachable, Aina needs to isolate **health posts** from the broader dataset of all health facilities.

trans-unit.source: File: `hotosm_mdg_health_facilities_points.gpkg` (or the respective GeoPackage you are using)
trans-unit.target: File: `hotosm_mdg_health_facilities_points.gpkg` (or the respective GeoPackage you are using)

trans-unit.source: Load it via drag and drop or through `Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`.
trans-unit.target: Load it via drag and drop or through `Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`.

trans-unit.source: **Open the attribute table** and check the column named `amenity`.
trans-unit.target: **Open the attribute table** and check the column named `amenity`.

trans-unit.source: **Filter by expression** to keep only health posts:
trans-unit.target: **Filter by expression** to keep only health posts:

trans-unit.source: Right-click the filtered layer in the Layers Panel → `Export` → `Save Features As…`
trans-unit.target: Right-click the filtered layer in the Layers Panel → `Export` → `Save Features As…`

trans-unit.source: **Remove the filter** or original layer from your project to avoid confusion.
trans-unit.target: **Remove the filter** or original layer from your project to avoid confusion.

trans-unit.source: 💡 **Tip**: Filtering directly in QGIS lets you work with a specific subset of features without modifying the original dataset.
trans-unit.target: 💡 **Tip**: Filtering directly in QGIS lets you work with a specific subset of features without modifying the original dataset.

trans-unit.source: 2. Load Isochrone Layers for the Three CRM Warehouses
trans-unit.target: 2. Load Isochrone Layers for the Three CRM Warehouses

trans-unit.source: Aina knows that only **three warehouses** stock the necessary medical supplies: **Antananarivo**, **Maroantsetra**, and **Tolanaro**. She will now load the isochrone layers for each of these warehouses to begin analyzing service areas.
trans-unit.target: Aina knows that only **three warehouses** stock the necessary medical supplies: **Antananarivo**, **Maroantsetra**, and **Tolanaro**. She will now load the isochrone layers for each of these warehouses to begin analyzing service areas.

trans-unit.source: **Load the individual isochrone layers** for each warehouse:
trans-unit.target: **Load the individual isochrone layers** for each warehouse:

trans-unit.source: You can drag and drop each file into QGIS or go to `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`.
trans-unit.target: You can drag and drop each file into QGIS or go to `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`.

trans-unit.source: **Inspect the attribute table** of each isochrone layer Confirm that each record has a `traveltime_h` field showing the estimated travel time in **hours**.
trans-unit.target: **Inspect the attribute table** of each isochrone layer Confirm that each record has a `traveltime_h` field showing the estimated travel time in **hours**.

trans-unit.source: **Remove all features where travel time is above 10 hours**:
trans-unit.target: **Remove all features where travel time is above 10 hours**:

trans-unit.source: **Export each filtered layer** to the `temp` folder : At this point, Aina also ensures all exported layers are saved in the same CRS as the health post dataset — `EPSG:4326` — to avoid problems in the spatial join.
trans-unit.target: **Export each filtered layer** to the `temp` folder : At this point, Aina also ensures all exported layers are saved in the same CRS as the health post dataset — `EPSG:4326` — to avoid problems in the spatial join.

trans-unit.source: **Style the isochrones for clarity** Aina can apply predefined style file to color the layer based on `traveltime_h` to visualize different time bands (4h, 6h, 8h, 10h) later in Step 5.
trans-unit.target: **Style the isochrones for clarity** Aina can apply predefined style file to color the layer based on `traveltime_h` to visualize different time bands (4h, 6h, 8h, 10h) later in Step 5.

trans-unit.source: 3. Visualizing Health Post Reachability from CRM Warehouses
trans-unit.target: 3. Visualizing Health Post Reachability from CRM Warehouses

trans-unit.source: Aina needs to identify which health posts can be reached by road from three key CRM warehouses (Antananarivo, Maroantsetra, and Tolanaro) **within 10 hours of travel time**. She will do this manually by combining the 10-hour isochrones from these warehouses and comparing them to the national health post dataset.
trans-unit.target: Aina needs to identify which health posts can be reached by road from three key CRM warehouses (Antananarivo, Maroantsetra, and Tolanaro) **within 10 hours of travel time**. She will do this manually by combining the 10-hour isochrones from these warehouses and comparing them to the national health post dataset.

trans-unit.source: **Merge the Isochrone Layers from the Three Warehouses**
trans-unit.target: **Merge the Isochrone Layers from the Three Warehouses**

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Merge Vector Layers`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Merge Vector Layers`.

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Select by Location`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Select by Location`.

trans-unit.source: 💡 The selected points are those within the 10-hour service areas of the warehouses.
trans-unit.target: 💡 The selected points are those within the 10-hour service areas of the warehouses.

trans-unit.source: **Create a Reachability Field for Selected Health Posts**
trans-unit.target: **Create a Reachability Field for Selected Health Posts**

trans-unit.source: Open the **Field Calculator** ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) on the `health_posts_only` layer.
trans-unit.target: Open the **Field Calculator** ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) on the `health_posts_only` layer.

trans-unit.source: Click `OK` to create and populate the new field for selected features.
trans-unit.target: Click `OK` to create and populate the new field for selected features.

trans-unit.source: **Mark the Remaining Health Posts as Not Reachable**
trans-unit.target: **Mark the Remaining Health Posts as Not Reachable**

trans-unit.source: Invert the selection: Go to `Edit` → `Invert Feature Selection` ![](/fig/mActionInvertSelection.png) or right-click the layer and select `Invert Selection`.
trans-unit.target: Invert the selection: Go to `Edit` → `Invert Feature Selection` ![](/fig/mActionInvertSelection.png) or right-click the layer and select `Invert Selection`.

trans-unit.source: ✅ Now all health posts are labeled as either **reachable** or **not reachable** in the `Reachability_time` column.
trans-unit.target: ✅ Now all health posts are labeled as either **reachable** or **not reachable** in the `Reachability_time` column.
